annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58190,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations README missing InvocationContext properties and manual DI setup","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/` **Triggered by:** commit 5dd1443c4 (PR #58178) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed Post-release version increment of `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` from `1.0.0-beta.1` → `1.0.0-beta.2` following the initial package release on 2026-04-14 as part of the major three-package architectural rewrite (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` beta.21). ## Gaps Found - `InvocationContext.InvocationId` is only mentioned in passing in the "Handler lifetime" section and is not described in the **InvocationContext** key concepts subsection — important since `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync` both receive an `invocationId` parameter that correlates with this value. - `InvocationContext.QueryParameters` (`IReadOnlyDictionary`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README. - `InvocationContext.Isolation` (`IsolationContext`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README — leaving multi-tenant scenarios (user/chat isolation keys) completely undocumented. - No manual ASP.NET Core DI setup example — the Responses README shows both `ResponsesServer.Run()` and a manual `WebApplication.CreateBuilder()` approach (`AddResponsesServer()` + `MapResponsesServer()`), but the Invocations README shows only `InvocationsServer.Run()`. The equivalent manual setup (`AddInvocationsServer()` + `MapInvocationsServer()`) is missing. - `InvocationsServerOptions` is part of the public API but is never mentioned or described in the README. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/README.md` — Edit #### 1a. Expand the `InvocationContext` key concept subsection Replace the existing `### InvocationContext` subsection (currently two sentences): ```markdown ### InvocationContext Provides request metadata to the handler, including the resolved session ID and forwarded client headers. ``` with: ```markdown ### InvocationContext Provides request metadata to the handler. All properties are read-only and resolved before `HandleAsync` is called. | Property | Type | Description | |----------|------|-------------| | `InvocationId` | `string` | Unique identifier for this invocation. Passed as the first argument to `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync`. Use as a key when storing per-invocation state. | | `SessionId` | `string` | Resolved multi-turn session identifier (extracted from query parameters or `INVOCATIONS_SESSION_ID` environment variable). | | `ClientHeaders` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | Forwarded `x-client-*` headers from the original request — useful for propagating tracing context and client metadata. | | `QueryParameters` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | All query parameters from the incoming request. | | `Isolation` | `IsolationContext` | Isolation context extracted from `x-agent-user-isolation-key` and `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` headers. Useful for multi-tenant scenarios where per-user or per-chat data must be isolated. `IsolationContext.Empty` indicates no isolation headers were present. | ``` #### 1b. Add a manual DI setup example in `### Configure the server` After the existing Tier 1 snippet block and `EchoHandler` code block (after the closing paragraph about `AgentHostBuilder`), add a new subsection: ```markdown For full control over the ASP.NET Core host, register services manually in your `Program.cs`: ``` var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); builder.Services.AddInvocationsServer(); builder.Services.AddScoped(); var app = builder.Build(); app.MapInvocationsServer(); app.Run(); ``` ``` #### 1c. Document `InvocationsServerOptions` in the `### Customizing the host` subsection Append after the existing tier sample links: ```markdown To configure `InvocationsServerOptions` (for future options), pass a configure delegate: ``` InvocationsServer.Run(configure: builder => { builder.AddInvocations(options => { // InvocationsServerOptions configuration here }); }); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets #### Snippet: `Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup` **Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` Add a new test method to the existing `ReadMeSnippets.cs` file: ```csharp [Test] public async Task ManualSetupSnippet() { #region Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); builder.Services.AddInvocationsServer(); builder.Services.AddScoped(); var app = builder.Build(); app.MapInvocationsServer(); app.Run(); #endregion #if !SNIPPET // Test-only: just verify compilation; don't actually run await Task.CompletedTask; #endif } ``` **README placeholder location:** In the `### Configure the server` subsection of `README.md`, add immediately after the `EchoHandler` class snippet: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The existing README already contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing Missing/incomplete subsections to update: - `### InvocationContext` — update per Step 1a above - `### Configure the server` — add manual setup per Step 1b above Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirms README is updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if any public types changed (no public API changes expected here) 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24482171351/agentic_workflow) · ● 1.4M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58190","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-15T22:51:28Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T22:51:28Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A bot-generated documentation issue identifying gaps in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` README — specifically missing `InvocationContext` property documentation (`InvocationId`, `QueryParameters`, `Isolation`) and a missing manual ASP.NET Core DI setup example with `AddInvocationsServer()`/`MapInvocationsServer()`.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` `1.0.0-beta.2` (post-release version bump from beta.1)
**Affected API:** `InvocationContext` (properties: `InvocationId`, `QueryParameters`, `Isolation`/`IsolationContext`), `InvocationsServerOptions`, `AddInvocationsServer()`, `MapInvocationsServer()`
**Scenarios:**
- Developers reading the README cannot learn what `InvocationId` is or how it correlates to `GetAsync`/`CancelAsync` parameters
- Multi-tenant scenarios using `IsolationContext` are completely undocumented
- `QueryParameters` on `InvocationContext` is not mentioned anywhere
- No manual DI setup example — only `InvocationsServer.Run()` shortcut is shown
- `InvocationsServerOptions` is in the public API but undescribed in the README

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/README.md` to fully document `InvocationContext` properties, add a manual DI setup example, and describe `InvocationsServerOptions`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue (no runtime bug). The gaps were identified by automated comparison with the Responses package README, which documents an equivalent `ResponseContext` with a richer section covering all properties.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/README.md` — confirm the `### InvocationContext` subsection covers only session ID and client headers
2. Check `InvocationContext` public API (e.g., `api/` listings or the source) to verify `InvocationId`, `QueryParameters`, and `Isolation` are public properties
3. Verify `AddInvocationsServer()` and `MapInvocationsServer()` extension methods exist in the package as counterparts to `AddResponsesServer()`/`MapResponsesServer()`
4. Cross-reference `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` for parallel documentation structure to guide the update
5. After edits, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to confirm snippet references resolve correctly
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — This is an SDK documentation issue for a data-plane client library (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations`), not a management-plane or provisioning package
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — The package lives under `sdk/agentserver/`, which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS (line 85)
- **Confidence:** High — The package name and service directory are explicitly stated in the issue body; the CODEOWNERS mapping is unambiguous; category "Client" is clear from the non-management, non-provisioning package naming
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — The issue's service label "AI Agents" matches this entry; no more specific multi-label entry for `%AI Agents` exists in the file
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko @RaviPidaparthi (line 86)
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners path); "needs-team-attention" not added because the issue is bot-reported (not "customer-reported")
- **Scan notes:** Scanned from end of file (line 1228) upward; all management-specific ServiceLabel entries (lines 900+) require `%Mgmt` which is not in the predicted labels; the `%AI Model Inference` entry at line 94 was not matched because the service label is "AI Agents" not "AI Model Inference"; the `%AI Agents` entry at line 85 was the first (and only) match
</details>
